feed.title: Babilonia - probabilistic coinjoin and covert betting

entry.title: Babilonia - probabilistic coinjoin and covert betting
entry.summary:
The modification of the transaction settlement process detailed in the correspondence suggests several key improvements to enhance security and efficiency. By restructuring the order in which transactions are signed and verified, it is proposed that the current vulnerability during the adaptor round could be eliminated. This involves pre-signing the RefundTx before any money moves, thereby ensuring that all proofs are verified and all signatures are exchanged before funds are transferred. This adjustment ensures that a failed proof or signature check would lead to an abort with no financial footprint.
The introduction of "flip channels" further innovates the system by allowing multiple bet flips within a single channel, where balances are maintained in a pre-signed state, and stakes move into an in-flight output during each flip. This mechanism relies on critical freshness per flip to prevent exploitation from reused thimble pairs, thus enhancing both the security and privacy of transactions. The enforcement mechanisms remain unchanged; however, the implementation requires careful management of transaction order to ensure that stale states are revoked appropriately before revealing any transitional keys.
Moreover, the email outlines a method for routing bets between parties who do not share a direct channel, utilizing PTLC-capable channels. This involves a sequence where the dealer first sends a combined stake locked to a certain timeout, followed by the player committing his stake under a shorter timeout. This ordering is crucial as it prevents the dealer from unfairly claiming the stake prior to the establishment of the pot, ensuring fairness and security in the transactions.
These proposals aim to refine transaction protocols to minimize risks and maximize the integrity and privacy of the betting mechanisms. By meticulously verifying every element before any financial commitment, along with strategic ordering and revocation of states, the system is poised to reduce vulnerabilities and enhance user trust in the transaction framework.
